Andy Mendelsohn

Cell cycle and apoptosis

amendelsohn@molsci.org
510-981-8731 (w)

Roles
Research Research@MSI

Biography
Education:
B.A. University of Pennsylvania in Biophysics and Natural Science in 1981
Ph.D. in Molecular and Cellular Biology at Brown University in 1991 studying how master genes control myogenesis and cell proliferation.
Ph.D. thesis title "Inhibition of myogenesis in vitro, characterization of effects of oncogene transformation."

Professional Experience:
Postdoctoral work investigating cellular logic and control of the cell cycle and apoptosis at Massachusetts General Hospital Department of Molecular Biology with Roger Brent (1991-1998)
Joined the Institute in 1998

Current Research:

Current research interests are evolutionary mechanisms of cellular maturation and death.

Publications
Mendelsohn, Andrew R, Hamer, Janice D, Wang, Zhaoyuan Bill, Brent, Roger. Cyclin D3 activates Caspase 2, connecting cell proliferation with cell death. PNAS 2002, 99, 6871-6876 PubMed
Mendelsohn, A R, Brent, R. Protein interaction methods--toward an endgame. Science 1999, 284, 1948-1950 PubMed
Mendelsohn, A R, Brent, R. Postgenomic protein analysis: the next bend in the river. Nat Biotechnol 1998, 16, 520-521 PubMed
Xu, C W, Mendelsohn, A R, Brent, R. Cells that register logical relationships among proteins. PNAS 1997, 94, 12473-12478 PubMed
Mendelsohn, A R, Brent, R. Applications of interaction traps/two-hybrid systems to biotechnology research. Curr Opin Biotechnol 1994, 5, 482-486 PubMed
